children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this part is to assist State educational agencies in reforming and improving their systems for personnel preparation and professional development in early intervention, educational, and transition services in order to improve results for children with disabilities.

children.heading: Definition of personnel
children.label: b Definition of personnel
children.text: In this part the term “personnel” means special education teachers, regular education teachers, principals, administrators, related services personnel, paraprofessionals, and early intervention personnel serving infants, toddlers, preschoolers, or children with disabilities, except where a particular category of personnel, such as related services personnel, is identified.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (d), for any fiscal year for which the amount appropriated under , that remains after the Secretary reserves funds under subsection (e) for the fiscal year, is less than $100,000,000, the Secretary shall award grants, on a competitive basis, to State educational agencies to carry out the activities described in the State plan submitted under .section 1455 of this titlesection 1453 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: are in States with the greatest personnel shortages; or

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrate the greatest difficulty meeting the requirements of .section 1412(a)(14) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not less than $500,000, nor more than $4,000,000, in the case of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than $80,000 in the case of an outlying area.

children.heading: Increase in amount
children.label: 4 Increase in amount
children.text: The Secretary may increase the amounts of grants under paragraph (4) to account for inflation.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of funds available for making the grants;

children.label: B
children.text: the relative population of the State or outlying area;

children.label: C
children.text: the types of activities proposed by the State or outlying area;

children.label: D
children.text: the alignment of proposed activities with ;section 1412(a)(14) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: the alignment of proposed activities with the State plans and applications submitted under sections 6311 and 6611(d) respectively, of this title; and1So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.1

children.label: F
children.text: the use, as appropriate, of scientifically based research activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), for the first fiscal year for which the amount appropriated under , that remains after the Secretary reserves funds under subsection (e) for the fiscal year, is equal to or greater than $100,000,000, and for each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary shall allot to each State educational agency, whose application meets the requirements of this part, an amount that bears the same relation to the amount remaining as the amount the State received under for that fiscal year bears to the amount of funds received by all States (whose applications meet the requirements of this part) under for that fiscal year.section 1455 of this titlesection 1411(d) of this titlesection 1411(d) of this title

children.heading: Minimum allotments for States that received competitive grants
children.label: 2 Minimum allotments for States that received competitive grants

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amount allotted under this subsection to any State educational agency that received a competitive multi-year grant under subsection (c) for which the grant period has not expired shall be not less than the amount specified for that fiscal year in the State educational agency’s grant award document under that subsection.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: Each such State educational agency shall use the minimum amount described in subparagraph (A) for the activities described in the State educational agency’s competitive grant award document for that year, unless the Secretary approves a request from the State educational agency to spend the funds on other activities.

children.label: A
children.text: the greater of $500,000 or ½ of 1 percent of the total amount available under this subsection for that year, in the case of each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: B
children.text: $80,000, in the case of an outlying area.

children.heading: Direct benefit
children.label: 4 Direct benefit
children.text: In using grant funds allotted under paragraph (1), a State educational agency shall, through grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements, undertake activities that significantly and directly benefit the local educational agencies in the State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this part, from funds appropriated under for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall reserve the amount that is necessary to make a continuation award to any State educational agency (at the request of the State educational agency) that received a multi-year award under this subchapter (as this subchapter was in effect on the day before ), to enable the State educational agency to carry out activities in accordance with the terms of the multi-year award.section 1455 of this titleDecember 3, 2004

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 2 Prohibition
children.text: A State educational agency that receives a continuation award under paragraph (1) for any fiscal year may not receive any other award under this part for that fiscal year.
